title: Alternative operators should be avoided
description: Alternative operators should not be usedAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEpitfallWhy is this an issue?Even though the C++ standard defines both "Primary" and "Alternative" operators, it isnota good idea to use the alternatives. Developers seeing an alphabetical name expect a variable, a function, a class, a namespace…​ in short, anything but an operator, and they will be confused at best by code that uses such operators.PrimaryAlternative&&and&=and_eq&bitand|bitor~compl!not!=not_eq||or|=or_eq^xor^=xor_eqNoncompliant code exampleif (not valid or error) { // Noncompliant /* ... */ }Compliant solutionif (!valid || error) { /* ..
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Alternative operators should be avoided. Use primary operators (!,&&,||,^,&,|,~,!=,&=,|=,^=) instead.
